composer: Shinya Sakamoto, Kazuki Muraoka, Atsushi Fujio, Kiyohiro Sada

voice_samples.pass: "It's a pass" or "Get the pass" (arcade version clearer)
voice_samples.damage_vocalization: "Aaahhh!" (losing player after fight or collision with goalie)

audio_identity.overall_style: 8-bit sports game with arcade-derived design, featuring distinctive digitized voice samples rare for NES at the time
distinctive_elements: Digitized voice samples (exclusive to cartridge release)
Multiple distinct voice callouts for gameplay events
Arcade version with greater variety of sound bites and commentary
Fast-paced action audio design matching hockey gameplay

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Blades of Steel (NES, Famicom Disk System)
notes: Documentation does not provide specific musical parameters (tempo, keys, chord progressions, time signatures, melodic analysis, or energy levels per track). Sources focus on gameplay mechanics and voice samples. NES cartridge version includes digitized voice samples; FDS version lacks these features. Arcade version has enhanced audio compared to NES/FDS ports.
